mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly compressing required returns and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.41% as of 2016-11-01 indicates near-zero rates post-crisis
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above a 0.3 percentage point monthly increase signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices reprice downward risk.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% as of 2016-11-01 indicates tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating housing starts signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, indicating strong aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST growth > 5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 465513M and HOUST at 1323K as of 2016-10-01
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during joint RSAFS and HOUST acceleration periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over a 3-month period signals weakening supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing activity, indicating economic slack that constrains profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite industrial production decline, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with declining unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that increase wage pressure and corporate labor costs, reducing profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity prices reprice lower earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth > 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 145128K and UNRATE at 4.6% as of 2016-11-01
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage inflation risk
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns during joint strong payroll and declining unemployment periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation as measured by CPI combined with elevated federal funds rates signals monetary tightening that increases real discount rates and reduces present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 242.35 and FEDFUNDS at 0.41% as of 2016-11-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during joint inflation and rate elevation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.035 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as equity prices reprice higher earnings expectations and economic momentum.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns during periods of above-trend GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below 1000K units signals weakening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic pessimism, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as equity prices immediately reprice downward growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines below 1000K units or falls > 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1323K as of 2016-10-01 indicates strong housing activity
regime_hint: housing market deterioration or demand shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite housing starts collapse, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
